title: Clarify proof fn vs macro security distinction
description: A reviewer raised: 'confused how you think macro_rules! is a security problem but proof-checking isn't. They're basically equivalent in what kind of AST-slinging happens at compile time.' The docs need a clear explanation of the distinction: proof fn is constrained to a verified logical language, erased after verification, cannot produce arbitrary code; macros can generate arbitrary unaudited code and expand attack surface. Add this to ferrum-introduction-to-proofs.md and/or ferrum-lang-core.md.
